job.aiSummaryShort: In diesem Job entwickelst du innovative Machine Learning-Ansätze zur Analyse von physiologischen Daten, erstellst Software in Python und unterstützt Forschungsprojekte sowie Lehrtätigkeiten an der Hochschule.

children.text: Completed Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Media Technology, Software Engineering or a related technical field that qualifies you for admission to a PhD/doctoral programme.
Advanced knowledge in the areas of explainable and interactive machine learning is an advantage.
Strong interest in applied research and emerging technologies.
Ability to work both independently and as part of a team.
Analytical thinking and a structured working style.
Initial research experience as an intern, teaching assistant or research assistant is desirable.
Strong programming skills in Python and experience with frameworks such as TensorFlow/PyTorch.
Experience with physiological or human-centered data, such as gait analysis, eye-tracking or EEG data, is an advantage.

A long-term employment in a modern, flexible work environment at the University of Applied Sciences St. Pölten with various work opportunities (e.g., remote work, creative spaces, etc.) as well as numerous benefits and further trainings.
More free time for our staff: additional special leave days from 24/12 to 31/12 as well as on Good Friday.
Permanent contract in an innovative, open-minded, and internationally oriented environment.
A gross annual salary that takes your qualifications into account: at least € 45.640,- (basis: 40 h/week) with flexitime option (no core time) and comp time.
You contribute to basic and applied research projects in the fields of machine learning, computer vision and explainable AI.
You develop and evaluate machine learning approaches for physiological or human-centered data, such as gait analysis, eye-tracking and EEG data.
You develop software in Python using common machine learning frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow and PyTorch).
You support the development of research prototypes and frontend applications for demonstrators.
You contribute to publications and the dissemination of research results.
To a limited extent, you are involved in teaching (max. 6 semester periods a week) as well as in supervising scientific theses and teaching projects.

job.responsibilitiesExcerpt: You contribute to basic and applied research projects in the fields of machine learning, computer...

jobDescriptionHtml.html:
Deine Rolle im Team

  • You contribute to basic and applied research projects in the fields of machine learning, computer vision and explainable AI.
  • You develop and evaluate machine learning approaches for physiological or human-centered data, such as gait analysis, eye-tracking and EEG data.
  • You develop software in Python using common machine learning frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow and PyTorch).
  • You support the development of research prototypes and frontend applications for demonstrators.
  • You contribute to publications and the dissemination of research results.
  • To a limited extent, you are involved in teaching (max. 6 semester periods a week) as well as in supervising scientific theses and teaching projects.

Unser Angebot

  • A long-term employment in a modern, flexible work environment at the University of Applied Sciences St. Pölten with various work opportunities (e.g., remote work, creative spaces, etc.) as well as numerous benefits and further trainings.
  • More free time for our staff: additional special leave days from 24/12 to 31/12 as well as on Good Friday.
  • Permanent contract in an innovative, open-minded, and internationally oriented environment.
  • A gross annual salary that takes your qualifications into account: at least € 45.640,- (basis: 40 h/week) with flexitime option (no core time) and comp time.

Technologien und Skills

  • Python
  • PyTorch
  • TensorFlow

Unsere Erwartungen an dich:

Qualifikationen

  • Advanced knowledge in the areas of explainable and interactive machine learning is an advantage.
  • Strong interest in applied research and emerging technologies.
  • Ability to work both independently and as part of a team.
  • Analytical thinking and a structured working style.

Erfahrung

  • Initial research experience as an intern, teaching assistant or research assistant is desirable.
  • Strong programming skills in Python and experience with frameworks such as TensorFlow/PyTorch.
  • Experience with physiological or human-centered data, such as gait analysis, eye-tracking or EEG data, is an advantage.

Ausbildung

  • Completed Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Media Technology, Software Engineering or a related technical field that qualifies you for admission to a PhD/doctoral programme.

Benefits

  • Home Office
  • Flexible Arbeitszeiten
  • Gesundheitsförderung
  • Kinderbetreuung

Programmierung und Betreuung langfristiger Kundenprojekte und Beteiligung an Produktentwicklung. Dabei gibt es einerseits Aufgaben mit Frontend-Fokus (CSS, JavaScript, Blazor), andererseits auch im Backend (performante, skalierbare Applikationen mit...

Build and ship new features across our Angular-based Cockpit and donation form systems Write clean, tested, well-documented code that your teammates enjoy working with Integrate with REST APIs and collaborate with backend engineers on data flows...

pageOverview.translatedPlatformText: Mit mehr als 3.700 Studierenden in sechs Departments ist die Fachhochschule St. Pölten ein wesentlicher Player in der heimischen Hochschullandschaft und zudem einer der wichtigsten Arbeitgeber in der Region.

meta.title: Junior Researcher - Machine Learning & AI bei FH St. Pölten | DEVjobs.at
meta.description: FH St. Pölten in St. Pölten sucht einen Junior Researcher - Machine Learning & AI mit PyTorch, Python und TensorFlow Kenntnissen. Jetzt bei DEVjobs.at bewerben!
